name: Barbell Squat
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor leaning against the rack upright, about 2 meters away at a 45-degree angle.
stepByStepCues: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Brace your core and take a deep breath
Push your knees out in line with your toes as you descend
Lower until thighs are parallel to the floor or below
Drive through your heels to stand back up

formRules.debriefCue: Your knees were caving inward on several reps — focus on pushing them out in line with your toes.
You're not reaching full depth — aim for hips below knee level on the next set.

name: Bicep Curl
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the bench beside you at waist height, camera facing your side.
stepByStepCues: Stand tall, core braced, shoulders back
Hold dumbbells with palms facing forward
Curl up keeping elbows pinned to your sides
Squeeze at the top, then lower slowly over 3 seconds
Fully extend at the bottom before the next rep

formRules.debriefCue: You're swinging your torso to lift the weight — pin your elbows to your sides and slow down.
Your elbows drifted forward on the curl — keep them pinned at your sides throughout the movement.

name: Lateral Raise
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the dumbbell rack shelf at chest height, camera facing you directly.
stepByStepCues: Stand with slight bend in elbows, dumbbells at your sides
Raise arms out to the sides, leading with your elbows
Stop when arms reach shoulder height — parallel to floor
Keep shoulders depressed — don't shrug
Lower slowly with control

formRules.debriefCue: Your traps are taking over — consciously pull your shoulders down away from your ears before each rep.
You're leaning to one side — stand upright and lower the weight if needed.

name: Deadlift
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor leaning against a weight plate, about 2 meters to your side.
stepByStepCues: Stand with bar over mid-foot, hip-width stance
Hinge at hips, grip bar just outside legs
Chest up, lats engaged, neutral spine before lifting
Push floor away, keep bar close to your body
Lock out hips and knees at the top simultaneously

formRules.debriefCue: Your lower back rounded during the pull — reset, brace your core hard, and think chest up before you lift.

formRules.description: Grip loosening — fingers opening under load
formRules.debriefCue: Your grip was opening during the pull — crush the bar harder or consider chalk/straps for heavier sets.

name: Bench Press
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor at the head of the bench, camera facing up toward you.
stepByStepCues: Lie with eyes under the bar, feet flat on floor
Grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists straight
Retract shoulder blades and arch slightly
Lower bar to lower chest with control
Press bar up and slightly back toward the rack

formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are flaring too wide — tuck them to about 60 degrees from your torso to protect your shoulders.

name: Overhead Press
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the bench beside you at waist height, camera facing your side.
stepByStepCues: Stand with bar at collarbone, grip shoulder-width
Brace core hard, squeeze glutes
Press bar straight up, moving head back slightly
Lock out fully at the top
Lower under control to collarbone

formRules.debriefCue: You're hyperextending your lower back — squeeze your glutes and brace your core before each press.

name: Barbell Row
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor about 2 meters to your side, leaning against a plate.
stepByStepCues: Hinge to 45-degree torso angle, bar hanging
Pull bar to lower chest, driving elbows back
Squeeze shoulder blades at the top
Lower under control — full extension at bottom
Keep torso angle consistent throughout

formRules.debriefCue: You're jerking the weight up — pause at the bottom of each rep and pull with control.

formRules.description: Grip loosening — fingers opening at the pull
formRules.debriefCue: Your grip was slipping during the row — focus on squeezing the bar hard before you initiate the pull.

name: Cable Tricep Pushdown
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor leaning against a weight plate, about 2 meters to your side.
stepByStepCues: Stand close to cable, elbows pinned to sides at 90 degrees
Push handle down until arms fully extended
Squeeze triceps hard at the bottom
Return slowly — stop when forearms are parallel to floor
Keep elbows locked in position throughout

formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are drifting forward — pin them to your sides and only your forearms should move.

name: Seated Cable Row
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the cable stack platform to your side at waist height.
stepByStepCues: Sit upright, slight bend in knees, neutral spine
Pull handle to lower abdomen, driving elbows back
Squeeze shoulder blades together at end position
Extend arms fully on the return — feel the lat stretch
Keep torso upright — don't rock back and forth

formRules.debriefCue: You're rocking back to pull the weight — keep your torso fixed at 90 degrees and pull with your back, not your body.

name: Dumbbell Lunge
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on a bench in front of you at chest height, camera facing you.
stepByStepCues: Stand tall, dumbbells at sides
Step forward with one leg, lowering back knee toward floor
Front knee stays above ankle — don't let it drift forward
Push through front heel to return to standing
Alternate legs or complete all reps on one side

formRules.debriefCue: Your front knee is caving inward on the lunge — drive it out in line with your second toe.

name: Push-up
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, propped against a water bottle.
stepByStepCues: Start in a high plank: hands shoulder-width, body in a straight line
Lower chest until it nearly touches the floor
Keep elbows at roughly 45 degrees — not fully flared
Press through the palms to full extension
Maintain a rigid core throughout — no hip sag or pike

formRules.description: Elbow flare — elbows pointing too wide
formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are flaring too wide on the push-up — keep them at 45 degrees from your torso.
Your hips are sagging — brace your core hard and keep your body in a rigid straight line.

name: Pull-up
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor beneath the pull-up bar, leaning against a weight plate, camera angled up.
stepByStepCues: Hang with arms fully extended, shoulders packed down
Pull elbows toward your hips, not just down
Chin clears the bar at the top
Depress your shoulder blades — don't let traps shrug
Lower slowly to full hang between every rep

formRules.description: Shoulder shrug — traps elevating at the top
formRules.debriefCue: Your shoulders are shrugging up at the top of the pull-up — actively depress your scapula as you pull.

name: Tricep Dip
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone 2 meters to your side on the floor, leaning against a dumbbell.
stepByStepCues: Grip parallel bars, arms locked out, shoulders down
Lean slightly forward for tricep emphasis
Lower until upper arms are parallel to the floor
Keep elbows tracking backward — not flaring out
Press back to lockout squeezing the triceps

formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are flaring out on the dip — keep them pointing straight back to keep the load on your triceps.

name: Incline Bench Press
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone at the foot end of the incline bench on the floor, angled up toward you.
stepByStepCues: Set bench to 30–45 degrees, feet flat on floor
Grip slightly wider than shoulder-width
Retract shoulder blades into the pad
Lower bar to upper chest with elbows at 60 degrees
Press up and slightly toward the rack

formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are flaring on the incline press — tuck them to protect your shoulder joint.

name: Dumbbell Row
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the bench beside you at waist height, camera facing your working side.
stepByStepCues: Place one hand and knee on the bench for support
Back is flat and parallel to the floor
Pull dumbbell to your hip, driving elbow back
Squeeze the lat at the top — pause briefly
Lower slowly with full arm extension at the bottom

formRules.description: Torso rotation — using body to swing weight
formRules.debriefCue: You're rotating your torso to lift the dumbbell — keep hips and shoulders square to the bench throughout.

name: Leg Press
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the seat beside you or prop it against the machine frame to your side.
stepByStepCues: Place feet shoulder-width on the platform, toes slightly out
Lower the platform until knees reach 90 degrees
Keep knees in line with your toes throughout
Push through the full foot — heels and toes
Don't lock out knees completely at the top

formRules.debriefCue: Your knees are caving inward on the leg press — push them out in line with your toes.

name: Romanian Deadlift
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor leaning against a plate, about 2 meters to your side.
stepByStepCues: Stand hip-width, bar or dumbbells at hip height
Push hips back, maintaining a long neutral spine
Lower weight along your legs until you feel hamstring tension
Drive hips forward to return — squeeze glutes at the top
Keep shoulders retracted throughout the movement
commonMistakes: Bending at the knees too much

formRules.debriefCue: Your back is rounding on the RDL — push your chest up and hinge from the hips, not the lower back.

name: Goblet Squat
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor leaning against a dumbbell, about 2 meters away at 45 degrees.
stepByStepCues: Hold dumbbell or kettlebell at chest, elbows pointing down
Feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, toes out
Squat down keeping chest tall and weight on heels
Drive knees out over toes throughout the descent
Stand by driving through the floor and extending hips

formRules.debriefCue: Your knees caved on the goblet squat — push them out and try a slightly wider stance.
You're not hitting depth on the goblet squat — this movement allows great depth so aim for hips below knees.

name: Hip Thrust
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, leaning against a plate.
stepByStepCues: Upper back against bench, feet flat on floor hip-width
Bar or plate across hip crease with padding
Drive through heels to thrust hips toward ceiling
Squeeze glutes hard at the top — body forms a straight line
Lower under control until just above the floor

formRules.description: Lumbar hyperextension at top of thrust
formRules.debriefCue: You're overextending your lower back at the top — squeeze your glutes and tuck your pelvis for a neutral spine.

name: Face Pull
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor or a bench 2 meters to your side at waist height.
stepByStepCues: Set cable at or above head height with rope attachment
Step back, arms extended, slight lean away from cable
Pull rope to face level, elbows finishing high and wide
Externally rotate at end — thumbs pointing back
Return slowly with full arm extension

formRules.debriefCue: Your traps are shrugging on the face pull — keep shoulders depressed and focus on pulling with rear delts.

name: Lat Pulldown
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the weight stack in front of you at chest height, camera facing you.
stepByStepCues: Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder-width
Lean back slightly, chest up, shoulders packed down
Pull bar to upper chest, driving elbows toward hips
Squeeze lats at the bottom — pause briefly
Return bar slowly to full arm extension

formRules.debriefCue: Your shoulders are shrugging up on the pulldown — depress your scapula first, then pull.

name: Cable Fly
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor in front of you, leaning against a weight plate, camera angled up.
stepByStepCues: Stand between cable towers, handles at chest height
Step forward slightly, staggered stance for balance
Arms extended with slight bend in elbows — maintain throughout
Bring handles together in a hugging arc
Squeeze chest at the peak, then return with control

formRules.description: Elbows bending excessively — turning into a press
formRules.debriefCue: You're bending your elbows too much — maintain a fixed slight bend and let the chest do the work.

name: Hammer Curl
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the bench beside you at waist height, camera facing your side.
stepByStepCues: Stand tall, dumbbells at sides with palms facing each other
Keep elbows pinned to your sides
Curl dumbbell up keeping neutral wrist position
Squeeze at the top, then lower under control
Fully extend at the bottom before the next rep

formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are drifting on the hammer curl — pin them to your sides for full brachialis engagement.

name: Skull Crusher
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, leaning against a plate.
stepByStepCues: Lie on bench, bar at arm's length above chest
Upper arms vertical — keep them there throughout
Lower bar by bending only at the elbows
Bar descends toward forehead or just behind
Extend back to start, squeezing triceps at the top

formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are flaring out — keep them pointing straight up and move only from the elbow joint.

name: Leg Extension
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the machine seat beside you or on the floor at your side.
stepByStepCues: Sit upright with back against pad, ankles behind the roller
Extend legs fully — squeeze quads hard at the top
Lower slowly over 3 seconds to starting position
Keep toes pointing forward, not flared
Avoid jerking — controlled movement only

formRules.debriefCue: Your knees are tracking outward on the leg extension — align them with your toes throughout.

name: Leg Curl
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, leaning against a weight plate.
stepByStepCues: Lie face down, ankles under the roller pad
Keep hips pressed firmly into the bench
Curl heels toward your glutes in a controlled arc
Squeeze hamstrings fully at the top
Lower slowly — don't slam the weight stack

formRules.debriefCue: Your hips are lifting on the leg curl — press them firmly into the pad and reduce the weight if needed.

name: Calf Raise
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, leaning against a dumbbell.
stepByStepCues: Stand with balls of feet on an elevated surface
Lower heels below platform level for a full stretch
Rise as high as possible onto the balls of your feet
Hold the peak contraction for 1 second
Lower slowly — 3 seconds down

formRules.debriefCue: You're leaning forward on the calf raise — keep your torso upright and let only your ankles move.

name: Front Raise
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the bench beside you at waist height, camera facing your side.
stepByStepCues: Stand tall, dumbbell in each hand at thigh height, palms down
Raise one or both arms to shoulder height
Keep a slight bend in the elbow throughout
Don't swing or lean back — control the weight
Lower slowly to starting position

formRules.debriefCue: You're leaning back to swing the weight up — lower the dumbbells and raise with strict shoulder control.

name: Reverse Fly
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, leaning against a plate.
stepByStepCues: Hinge forward 45–90 degrees, dumbbells hanging below chest
Keep a slight bend in the elbows throughout
Raise arms out to the sides leading with the elbows
Squeeze rear delts at the top — pause briefly
Lower under control, maintaining hinge angle

formRules.description: Shoulder shrugging — traps dominating the movement
formRules.debriefCue: Your traps are shrugging on the reverse fly — keep shoulders depressed and drive your elbows up and back.

name: Arnold Press
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the dumbbell rack at chest height, camera facing you directly.
stepByStepCues: Sit upright, dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing you
Rotate palms outward as you press up
Finish with palms facing forward at full extension
Reverse the rotation as you lower back to start
Keep core braced — no lower back arch

formRules.debriefCue: You're arching your lower back on the Arnold press — brace your core and squeeze your glutes throughout.

name: Jumping Jack
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on a stable surface facing you at chest height, about 2-3 meters away.
stepByStepCues: Stand with feet together, arms at your sides
Jump feet out to shoulder width while raising arms overhead
Jump back to starting position simultaneously
Keep a slight bend in your knees to cushion each landing
Maintain a steady, controlled rhythm throughout

name: Burpee
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on a stable surface facing you at chest height, about 2-3 meters away.
stepByStepCues: Stand tall, feet shoulder-width apart
Drop hands to the floor and jump feet back to a plank position
Perform a push-up (optional), then jump feet back to hands
Explosively jump up with arms overhead
Land softly with bent knees and immediately repeat
commonMistakes: Not fully extending at the top jump

name: Mountain Climber
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on a stable surface facing you at chest height, about 2-3 meters away.
stepByStepCues: Start in a high plank: wrists under shoulders, body in a straight line
Drive one knee toward your chest while keeping hips level
Quickly switch legs in a running motion
Keep core braced throughout — no hip bouncing
Maintain a steady pace, faster as you build endurance

name: High Knees
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on a stable surface facing you at chest height, about 2-3 meters away.
stepByStepCues: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart
Run in place, driving knees up to hip height
Pump arms in opposition to your legs
Land on the balls of your feet softly
Keep chest up and core engaged throughout

name: Jump Squat
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on a stable surface facing you at chest height, about 2-3 meters away.
stepByStepCues: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Squat down to parallel, keeping chest up and knees tracking toes
Explode upward, jumping as high as you can
Land softly with bent knees — absorb the impact quietly
Immediately descend into the next squat
commonMistakes: Not squatting to parallel before jumping

name: Sumo Deadlift
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor leaning against a weight plate, about 2 meters in front of you.
stepByStepCues: Stand wide with toes pointed out at 30–45 degrees
Grip the bar inside your legs, arms straight down
Chest up, lats tight, hips lower than conventional deadlift
Push the floor apart as you stand — drive knees out
Lock hips and knees simultaneously at the top

formRules.description: Knee valgus — knees caving inward on the pull
formRules.debriefCue: Your knees caved on the sumo deadlift — actively push them out in line with your toes as you pull.
Your back is rounding on the sumo pull — think chest up and brace hard before breaking the floor.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, leaning against a dumbbell.
stepByStepCues: Place rear foot on a bench, laces down
Step forward far enough that your front shin stays vertical
Lower your rear knee toward the floor with control
Keep torso upright — don't lean excessively forward
Drive through the front heel to stand
commonMistakes: Front foot too close to bench

formRules.debriefCue: Your front knee is caving on the split squat — drive it out in line with your second toe.

name: Close Grip Bench Press
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor at the head of the bench, camera angled up toward you.
stepByStepCues: Grip bar shoulder-width or slightly narrower, wrists straight
Unrack with elbows close to your sides
Lower bar to lower chest, keeping elbows tucked at 30 degrees
Press straight up, squeezing triceps at full extension
Keep shoulder blades retracted into the bench throughout
commonMistakes: Grip too narrow causing wrist pain

formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are flaring on the close grip press — keep them at 30 degrees to target the triceps effectively.

name: Chest Dip
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, leaning against a dumbbell.
stepByStepCues: Grip parallel bars, arms locked out, lean chest forward
Lean torso forward at 20–30 degrees for chest emphasis
Lower until elbows reach 90 degrees or upper arms are parallel to floor
Keep elbows tracking backward — not flaring to the sides
Press back to full lockout through the palms

formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are flaring on the chest dip — keep them pointing backward to protect your shoulder joint.

name: Concentration Curl
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on a bench in front of you at waist height, camera facing you.
stepByStepCues: Sit on a bench, elbow braced against your inner thigh
Dumbbell hanging at arm's length between your legs
Curl the dumbbell up, rotating palm outward at the top
Squeeze the bicep peak — hold briefly at the top
Lower slowly to full extension

formRules.debriefCue: You're rocking to lift the weight — keep your torso fixed and let only your elbow flex.

name: Preacher Curl
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, leaning against a plate.
stepByStepCues: Sit at preacher bench, upper arms flat on the pad
Grip EZ bar or dumbbell, arms nearly fully extended
Curl up without lifting shoulders off the pad
Squeeze at the top — full contraction
Lower slowly to near-full extension — don't hyperextend at the bottom

formRules.debriefCue: Your shoulders are rising on the preacher curl — keep them pinned to the pad for full bicep isolation.

name: Tricep Kickback
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the bench beside you at waist height, camera facing your side.
stepByStepCues: Hinge forward, upper arm parallel to the floor and pinned to side
Elbow at 90 degrees — this is the start position
Extend forearm back until arm is fully straight
Squeeze tricep hard at full extension
Return forearm slowly — upper arm stays fixed throughout

formRules.description: Upper arm dropping — elbow not staying parallel to floor
formRules.debriefCue: Your upper arm is dropping during the kickback — keep it parallel to the floor throughout the movement.

name: Seated Dumbbell Curl
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the dumbbell rack at chest height, camera facing you.
stepByStepCues: Sit upright on a bench, dumbbells hanging at sides
Curl both or alternating, keeping elbows fixed at your sides
Supinate wrists at the top — little finger rotates up
Squeeze biceps fully at peak contraction
Lower under control to full extension

formRules.debriefCue: You're swinging your torso — sit upright, keep your back against the pad, and curl with control.

name: Upright Row
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the dumbbell rack at chest height, camera facing you directly.
stepByStepCues: Stand with barbell or dumbbells at hip level, overhand grip
Pull elbows high and wide, leading the movement
Raise until elbows are at or above shoulder height
Bar or dumbbells stay close to the body throughout
Lower under control to starting position

formRules.description: Traps over-shrugging instead of deltoids driving
formRules.debriefCue: Your traps are dominating the upright row — focus on driving your elbows high and wide, not just lifting the bar.

name: Cable Lateral Raise
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the cable stack at chest height, camera facing you directly.
stepByStepCues: Stand beside the cable, handle in the far hand, cable crossing low
Slight bend in the elbow throughout the movement
Raise arm out to your side to shoulder height
Keep your torso upright — no side lean
Lower slowly, resisting the cable all the way down

formRules.description: Torso leaning away from the cable for momentum
formRules.debriefCue: You're leaning away to swing the cable up — stand upright and lift with shoulder strength only.

name: Cable Bicep Curl
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor leaning against a plate, 2 meters to your side.
stepByStepCues: Stand facing the low cable, handle in both hands underhand
Elbows pinned to sides at the start
Curl the handle up to chin level
Squeeze biceps at the top — cable maintains tension throughout
Lower slowly — the cable keeps constant load unlike free weights

formRules.description: Elbows drifting forward off your sides
formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are drifting forward on the cable curl — pin them to your sides for full bicep engagement.

name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor leaning against a plate, 2 meters to your side.
stepByStepCues: Stand facing high cable, bar in both hands, hinge slightly at hips
Arms extended with a very slight bend in the elbows
Pull bar down in an arc to your thighs, arms staying straight
Squeeze lats hard at the bottom — bar reaches hip level
Return slowly with full lat stretch at the top

formRules.description: Elbows bending — converting to a lat pulldown
formRules.debriefCue: Your elbows are bending on the straight-arm pulldown — keep arms nearly locked and pull with your lats.

name: Cable Crunch
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor leaning against a plate, 2 meters to your side.
stepByStepCues: Kneel facing the cable, rope attachment at high pulley
Hold rope beside your head, elbows pointing down
Crunch downward by flexing your spine — not your hips
Round your upper back to fully contract the abs
Return slowly — feel the stretch at the top
commonMistakes: Hinging at hips instead of flexing spine
Not contracting fully at the bottom

formRules.description: Hinging at hips instead of flexing spine
formRules.debriefCue: You're hinging at the hips not crunching — keep hips still and focus on rounding your spine.

name: Box Jump
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on the floor 2 meters to your side, leaning against a weight plate.
stepByStepCues: Stand in front of the box, feet shoulder-width
Load hips by sitting into a quarter squat
Swing arms and explode upward — land softly on the box
Land with knees bent, feet flat on the box
Step down one foot at a time — do not jump down

name: Step Up
phonePlacementInstruction: Place your phone on a stable surface facing you at chest height, about 2 meters away.
stepByStepCues: Stand in front of a bench or box at knee height
Step one foot fully onto the surface
Drive through the heel of the raised foot to stand
Keep chest tall — don't lean forward excessively
Step down with control and alternate legs
commonMistakes: Knee caving inward on the step

formRules.description: Knee caving inward on the step
formRules.debriefCue: Your knee is caving inward on the step up — drive it out in line with your second toe as you push up.
